description: Create a content slide titled 'The Binary Big Bang' with a full-width horizontal timeline showing key technology milestones from 1843 to 2032, separated into historical, emerging, and future phases.

header.kicker: Technology Vision 2025 | AI: A Declaration of Autonomy
header.headline: The Binary Big Bang

rows.bar_label: Ada Lovelace writes “Note G,” widely considered to be the first complex computer algorithm, on a sketch of Charles Babbage’s Analytical Engine
Researchers unveil ENIAC, the first general-purpose electronic computer
COBOL, a major programming language for business, finance, and administrative systems, is created
Alto, a computer with a graphical user interface, is developed at Xerox PARC
Sun Microsystems releases Java 1.0 as a ‘write once, run anywhere’ programming language
Amazon launches AWS, and 12,000 developers reportedly sign up on the first day
“Attention Is All You Need” paper proposes the Transformer architecture and paves the way for modern LLMs
Toolformer, an LLM that can teach itself to use external tools through APIs, is created
People regularly use personal agents to access data and complete tasks across digital ecosystems
A major ERP company launches a new agent-first platform design that lets users create custom data dashboards using natural language
An agent-powered startup goes public, and has the highest market cap-to-employee ratio in history
Agents, not people, are the primary users of most enterprises’ internal digital systems
Interacting with agents surpasses apps in average consumer time spent on smart devices
